tests.name: Stool RE Test
tests.description: Measures acidity or alkalinity of stool to identify digestive issues.

tests.name: Occult Blood Stool Test
tests.description: Detects hidden blood in stool samples.

tests.name: Stool Culture Test
tests.description: Checks bacterial infections in the digestive tract.

tests.name: Complete Blood Count with ESR (CBC + ESR)
tests.description: Measures overall health and detects disorders.

tests.name: Blood Sugar Test
tests.description: Checks glucose level in blood.

tests.name: Lipid Profile Test
tests.description: Measures cholesterol and triglyceride levels.

tests.name: Liver Function Test with GGT
tests.description: Evaluates liver health and function.

tests.name: Kidney Function Test with Electrolytes
tests.description: Checks kidney performance and health.

tests.name: Thyroid Profile Test
tests.description: Assesses thyroid hormone levels.

tests.name: Hemoglobin Test
tests.description: Measures hemoglobin concentration in blood.

tests.name: Routine Urine Test
tests.description: General urine analysis for infections and kidney issues.

tests.name: Urine Culture Test
tests.description: Detects urinary tract infections.

tests.name: Pregnancy Urine Test
tests.description: Detects pregnancy hormones.

tests.name: Sputum Culture Test
tests.description: Checks lung infections using sputum samples.

tests.name: X-Ray
tests.description: Imaging test for bones and chest diagnosis.

tests.name: Ultrasound
tests.description: Diagnostic imaging using sound waves.

tests.name: Echocardiography
tests.description: Ultrasound imaging for heart diagnosis.

tests.name: MRI (Magnetic Resonance Imaging)
tests.description: A medical imaging technique that uses magnetic fields and radio waves to create detailed images of organs, tissues, brain, spine, joints, and internal body structures.

tests.name: CT Scan (Computed Tomography Scan)
tests.description: An advanced imaging test that combines multiple X-ray images to create cross-sectional views of the body for detecting injuries, infections, tumors, and internal conditions.

tests.name: NCV (Nerve Conduction Velocity)
tests.description: A diagnostic test used to evaluate nerve damage and nerve function by measuring the speed of electrical signals through nerves.

tests.name: EEG (Electroencephalogram)
tests.description: A test that records electrical activity in the brain and helps diagnose epilepsy, seizures, sleep disorders, and other neurological conditions.

tests.name: EMG (Electromyography)
tests.description: A diagnostic procedure used to assess muscle health and nerve cells controlling muscles by measuring electrical activity.

tests.name: ECG (Electrocardiogram)
tests.description: A heart monitoring test that records the electrical activity of the heart to detect irregular heartbeat, heart disease, and cardiac abnormalities.

tests.name: BERA (Brainstem Evoked Response Audiometry)
tests.description: A hearing assessment test that evaluates the auditory nerve and brainstem response to sound, commonly used for infants and hearing disorder diagnosis.

tests.name: Doppler Study (Doppler Ultrasound Study)
tests.description: A non-invasive ultrasound test used to measure blood flow through blood vessels and detect blockages, clots, or circulation problems.
